channel.title: Kubai Kevin
channel.description: Practical backend engineering, AI tooling, and developer career advice by Kubai Kevin — a software developer with 10+ years building production systems in fintech and AI.

item.title: Fintech features break in Ghana: the real fix
item.description: Fintech features break in Ghana: hidden timeouts, ID formats, and rate limits that work in Lagos but fail in Accra — how to fix them without country forks and verify the fix in staging.

item.title: LangGraph vs LangChain: which LLM stack I ship…
item.description: LangGraph vs LangChain in 2026: which evaluation stack survives production traffic, keeps costs under control, and makes on-call sane? Cold-start numbers, memory footprints, and the one mistake that cost me three days.

item.title: Agents keep breaking prod: 3 boundary rules you still need
item.description: Agent breaking prod silently: 3 boundary rules to add now — outcome polling, schema drift checks, and concurrency guards — with concrete fixes and failure costs

item.title: Agentic workflows: how we kept the lights…
item.description: Agentic workflow observability with millisecond timestamps, queue depth alerts, and OpenSearch Serverless — the stack that cut our MTTR 93% and p99 latency 74%

item.title: Why progressive web apps fail on 2G and how to fix…
item.description: Cut 2G page load times from 12 seconds to 2 seconds — the real architecture moves that work in 2026 for React, PWA and mobile-first sites

item.title: Red-teaming agents without killing velocity
item.description: Cut security backlog 62% by embedding red-teaming in every PR — the lightweight setup that caught 12 incidents in one quarter without slowing releases.

item.title: AI features as tech debt or golden paths
item.description: AI features built with standard wrappers often become maintenance nightmares in Africa. Learn why caching, flags, and routing assumptions fail — and how to build resilient AI features that survive 3G drops and model drift.

item.title: Agents roll back without breaking anything
item.description: Rollbacks break downstream systems when agents leak state into caches, pools, and rate tables. Version everything — headers, queries, pool sizes — and treat rollbacks as blue-green activations, not reverts. Measure downstream metrics after every deploy.

item.title: Survived 12,000 agents with Redis
item.description: Cut API response time 60% with Redis caching — connection pooling, eviction policies, and the cache stampede mistake most teams make.

item.title: Offline-first agents for field teams
item.description: Offline-first agents cut last-mile failures 12% in Lagos pilots — SQLite encryption, conflict UI, and AWS AppSync for riders on 2G

item.title: Ownership drift after velocity spikes
item.description: Ownership drift after velocity spikes explained — how Redis 7.2 cache policy gates cut Sev-1s 3x and saved 120 engineer hours per quarter

item.title: Keep multi-model systems stable: 6 rules that worked
item.description: Cut multi-model agent costs 75% with idempotency keys and concurrency limits — Redis 7.2 eviction rates, Lambda arm64 cost, and the 503 error mistake most teams repeat

item.title: AI sales cycles: 3x faster, 40% less talk
item.description: Cut sales cycle time 66% with AI for dev tools — real code, failure modes, and the $8/lead cost that beats human reps. Includes Hugging Face 4.38, GitHub Copilot 1.84, and n8n 1.30 breakdown.

item.title: AI latency cut: 2.8s to 380ms without retraining
item.description: Cut AI feature latency from 2.8s to 420ms in production — the real reasons it spiked and how to verify the fix using Jaeger, Redis 7.2, and FastAPI 0.109.

item.title: GPU spot instances > reserved for AI costs
item.description: Cut GPU costs 42% with spot instances + retry logic — real AWS numbers, Python retry manager, and when to switch from reserved capacity in 2026.

item.title: HTMX + Islands beat React: our internal tools saved 40%…
item.description: Cut build time 40% with HTMX + Islands — measured latency, bundle sizes, and deployment wins for internal tools in 2026.

item.title: Stop guessing tech stacks: validate tools in 2 hours
item.description: Cut tool validation time 70% with load tests, tracing, and ADRs — avoid connection leaks, cache stampedes, and cold starts in staging before they hit production.

item.title: Trim LLM bills: 3 FinOps moves that work
item.description: Cut LLM bill 34% with 3 levers most teams ignore: semantic shaping, two-layer caching, and priority queues. Real numbers from a 12K-request/day system on AWS Bedrock and Claude 3.5.

item.title: Agent code reviews: 1,200 tests per PR
item.description: Cut agent review time from 2h 12m to 1m 52s with automated gates — static analysis, ephemeral tests, and risk-based escalation using checkov 3.2.117, Node 20 LTS, and AWS Lambda.

item.title: Replace vibe testing with agent evals now
item.description: Cut agent bugs 79% with automated evaluation loops — synthetic scenarios, rule checks, and LLM judges you can run in CI today. 2026 real numbers included.
